title: Add event on container death
body: First step of converting Wait over to use events

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Gets us an event formatted like this:

```
2019-03-12 16:25:27.088574667 -0400 EDT container died 70f01ba82a02575d3efc3579ee5951f1b3b3383243b204a54c21989e4b2d41fa (image=docker.io/library/fedora:latest, name=pensive_payne)
```

I matched Docker for the name but wouldn't nine changing to exited

On Wed, Mar 13, 2019, 07:33 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> *@rhatdan* commented on this pull request.
> ------------------------------
>
> In libpod/events.go
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2621#discussion_r265083782>:
>
> > @@ -19,6 +19,19 @@ func (c *Container) newContainerEvent(status events.Status) {
>  	}
>  }
>
> +// newContainerDiedEvent creates a new event for a container's death
> +func (c *Container) newContainerDiedEvent(exitCode int32) {
>
> Should we call this Exited rather then Died?
> ------------------------------
>
> In libpod/events/events.go
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2621#discussion_r265083839>:
>
> > @@ -58,6 +58,8 @@ const (
>  	Commit Status = "commit"
>  	// Create ...
>  	Create Status = "create"
> +	// Died indicates that a container's process died
> +	Died Status = "died"
>
> Exited.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2621#pullrequestreview-213895736>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCLRQ27xRxRcqReBkAfza9iG-lysrks5vWOIZgaJpZM4brzjR>
> .
>

I prefer "exited" too, but am just concerned about someone expecting 'died" due to a script that they run.  If that's a valid concern, I'd leave it as died.
Otherwise LGTM
Lets go with Exited since this is what happened.  Exited whether through successful running or failure.
